instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `74e4785` (“Move to Stanford”) from the reflog and merged it into `master`.

- Merge commit: `442d767`
- Preserved recovery branch: `recovered-stanford-changes`
- Working tree is clean
- Content and conflict-marker checks passed
- Jekyll build unavailable because Bundler is not installed (`bundle: not found`)

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
--- branches ---
' && git branch -avv && printf '
--- reflog ---
' && git reflog --date=iso -20 && printf '
--- recent graph ---
' && git log --all --graph --decorate --oneli

git show --stat --oneline --decorate 74e4785 && printf '
--- patch ---
' && git show --format=fuller --find-renames 74e4785 && printf '
--- master-only commit ---
' && git show --stat --oneline d7d3e4b

printf '%s
' '--- common base ---'; git show c4e38a1:_includes/about.md; printf '%s
' '
--- master ---'; cat _includes/about.md; printf '%s
' '
--- recovered ---'; git show 74e4785:_includes/about.md
ls -la | sed -n '1,100p'; printf '\n--- project config ---\n'; find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name 'Gemfile' -o -name 'package.json' -o -name 'Makefile' -o -name '*.yml' \) -print | sort
